summary.zh: 未嫁而死的女子所化之鬼，白衣散发，因怨恨滞留人间；密阳阿娘的冤魂吓死一任又一任府使，直到有人肯听她鸣冤。
summary.en: The ghost of a woman who died unmarried, white-robed with loose black hair, bound to the world by grievance; the wronged Arang of Miryang frightened magistrate after magistrate to death until one stayed to hear her.

description.zh:
处女鬼身穿白色素服，长发披散遮着惨白的脸，多在井边、老树下、空宅里现身。朝鲜时代的观念里，女子未嫁而死，无夫家奉祀，无子孙祭奠，魂魄进不了祖先的行列，只能滞留人间；若死于冤屈，怨气更重。民间的对策是替她办冥婚，或请巫觋做解冤的씻김굿，把缠住魂魄的结一段段解开。

最著名的处女鬼是密阳的阿娘。传说府使之女阿娘被乳母诱出，深夜在岭南楼畔赏月，遭衙役逼迫，不从被杀，尸身弃于竹林。此后新任府使夜夜被白衣女鬼惊死，密阳成了无人敢赴任的凶地，直到一位胆大的府使秉烛坐到深夜，听完她的冤情。翌日升堂，冤魂指认了凶手——一说化作白蝶落在凶手帽上——阿娘的骸骨从竹林中寻出安葬，岭南楼旁至今立着奉祀她的阿娘阁。朝鲜后期小说《蔷花红莲传》讲的是同一类故事：被继母构陷溺死的姐妹夜夜现身官衙鸣冤，吓死数任使道，终于等到一位肯问“你是谁”的官员。

这类故事的骨架是一场迟到的听证：鬼并不滥杀，她一遍遍回来，只因无人听完她的话。二十世纪以后处女鬼搬进了学校：厕所里的白衣女生、深夜自习室的长发身影，成为韩国校园怪谈的主角。1998年电影《女高怪谈》与2003年改编自《蔷花红莲传》的《蔷花，红莲》把这套形象送进了东亚恐怖片的共同记忆。
description.en:
The cheonyeo gwisin appears in a white mourning robe, long black hair hanging over a bloodless face, haunting wells, old trees, and empty houses. In Joseon thinking, a woman who died unmarried had no husband's line to enshrine her and no descendants to feed her spirit, so she could not join the ancestors and lingered in the world; if she had died wronged, the grievance bound her tighter. Folk remedies included posthumous ghost marriages and the ssitgim-gut, a shamanic rite that unties, knot by knot, the resentments holding a soul back.

The most famous of these ghosts is Arang of Miryang. A magistrate's daughter lured out by her nurse to view the moon by the Yeongnamnu pavilion, she was assaulted by a yamen servant, killed for resisting, and dumped in a bamboo grove. Every newly appointed magistrate was found dead of fright after his first night, and Miryang became a posting no one dared accept, until one official sat up with a candle and heard her story to the end. At the next morning's assembly the ghost identified the culprit, in some tellings as a white butterfly alighting on his hat. Her bones were recovered and buried, and the Arang-gak shrine still stands below the pavilion. The late Joseon novel Janghwa Hongnyeon jeon tells the same pattern: two sisters drowned through their stepmother's plot appear nightly at the magistrate's office until an official finally asks who they are.

The skeleton of these stories is a delayed hearing: the ghost returns again and again because no one has yet listened to the end. In the twentieth century she moved into schools, and the white-clad girl of the toilet stall and the night study hall became the stock figure of Korean campus horror, carried into East Asian cinema by Whispering Corridors (1998) and A Tale of Two Sisters (2003).

value.en: White mourning robe, long hair over the face

value.zh: 密阳阿娘传说、《蔷花红莲传》
